doc_bottomsheet_desc: Mobile-optimized panel sliding from the bottom of the screen
doc_bottomsheet_best_practice_1: Only use for mobile devices or narrow screens. For desktop, consider using Dialog or Drawer.
doc_bottomsheet_best_practice_2: Display a handle at the top to visually indicate that it can be closed by swiping or dragged.
doc_bottomsheet_best_practice_3: Ideally, keep it simple and focus on a single task or a small number of actions.
doc_bottomsheet_features_backdrop_desc: Darkens the background and blurs content to encourage visual focus.
doc_bottomsheet_features_focus_desc: Has a focus trap feature for accessibility.
doc_bottomsheet_features_handle_desc: Displays a visual handle conforming to common bottom sheet UI.
doc_bottomsheet_features_mobile_opt_desc: Positioned at the bottom, making it easy to reach with the thumb.
doc_bottomsheet_features_scroll_desc: Inner scroll area handles long content.
doc_bottomsheet_long_desc: A bottom sheet is an overlay component fixed to the bottom edge of the screen. Mostly used in mobile interfaces to display task-specific actions or additional info while keeping the current context.
doc_bottomsheet_mobile_only: > Smartphone only: This component is mainly designed for mobile devices. Try Dialog or Drawer for Desktop.
doc_bottomsheet_title: An overlay panel that slides up from the bottom of the screen, typically used on mobile devices.
doc_commandpalette_feature_1_desc: Instantly open or close the palette with shortcut keys like ⌘K (Ctrl+K).
doc_commandpalette_feature_2_desc: Filter commands, pages, and actions in real-time based on your input.
doc_commandpalette_feature_3_desc: Organize commands into categories using CommandPaletteGroup.
doc_commandpalette_long_desc_1: The Command Palette is a full-screen search UI that can be invoked with keyboard shortcuts like ⌘K.
doc_commandpalette_long_desc_2: Quickly search and execute actions, or navigate pages, entirely via the keyboard.
doc_commandpalette_role_1: Root component that manages the overall state of the palette.
doc_commandpalette_role_2: Trigger element that opens the palette.
doc_commandpalette_role_3: Content area where the palette is rendered.
doc_commandpalette_role_4: Text input field for searching commands.
doc_commandpalette_role_5: List that displays filtered search results.
doc_commandpalette_role_6: Container for grouping commands by category.
doc_commandpalette_role_8: Empty state displayed when no results match the search.
doc_commandpalette_role_9: Footer area at the bottom of the palette.
doc_commandpalette_usage_2: Standalone Usage as a Controlled Component
doc_dialog_a11y_1: Correctly manages focus on open/close (Focus Trapping).
doc_dialog_a11y_2: Root element uses role='dialog' or role='alertdialog'.
doc_dialog_a11y_3: Linked to titles and descriptions via aria-labelledby and aria-describedby.
doc_dialog_a11y_4: Supports closing the dialog via the Escape key.
doc_dialog_alert_dialog_desc: Use for critical notifications requiring user confirmation (e.g., confirming a deletion).
doc_dialog_basic_usage_desc: Standard implementation of the Dialog component, triggered by a button or other interaction.
doc_dialog_best_practice_1: Keep actions within the dialog clear and use predictable labels (e.g., Save/Discard instead of OK/Cancel).
doc_dialog_best_practice_2: Avoid displaying another dialog inside a dialog (nesting), as it confuses users.
doc_dialog_controlled_desc: Example of managing the open state using an external value.
doc_dialog_custom_content_desc: Inject arbitrary JSX into the dialog body for complex forms or information displays.
doc_dialog_design_philosophy_desc: Principles of interaction design centered on clear user intent.
doc_dialog_eliminate_ambiguity_desc: Ensure the user knows exactly what action the dialog will take before they confirm.
doc_dialog_guideline_1: Use when you need to ask for confirmation, prompt for important input, or interrupt the user's task to demand attention.
doc_dialog_guideline_2: When displaying a modal, darken the background and disable interactions behind it to focus the user's attention on the current action.
doc_dialog_long_desc: Details and usage for the Dialog component.
doc_dialog_ooui_consistency_desc: Maintain consistency with Object-Oriented User Interface principles for intuitive behavior.
doc_dialog_size_variations_desc: Control the dialog width using properties like size='sm', 'md', 'lg', or 'xl'.
doc_dialog_uncontrolled_desc: Example where the component manages its own open state internally.
doc_dialog_why_not_modal_desc: 'Modal' is an adjective describing a state where interaction is restricted. By using the proper name 'Dialog', we more accurately represent the UI component.
doc_dialog_with_header_footer_desc: Structure complex dialogs using Dialog.Header for titles and Dialog.Footer for primary actions.
doc_drawer_basic_principles_desc: Core guidelines for using drawers effectively in complex workflows.
doc_drawer_basic_usage_desc: Standard implementation of the Drawer component for side-out panels.
doc_drawer_controlled_desc: Managing the open/close state from parent components for custom logic.
doc_drawer_long_desc: Detailed information and usage instructions for the Drawer component.
doc_drawer_note_main_action_desc: Keep primary actions reachable within the drawer content area.
doc_drawer_note_mobile_desc: Adaptive design considerations for small-screen interactions.
doc_drawer_scene_desktop_desc: Typical usage patterns for side-panels in desktop applications.
doc_drawer_scene_future_desc: Advanced interaction patterns for upcoming complex feature sets.
doc_drawer_scene_multi_level_desc: Efficiently handling nested menus or deep information architectures.
doc_drawer_type_modal_desc: A modal drawer that provides a backdrop and focuses user attention.
doc_drawer_type_standard_desc: Displayed in parallel with main content without a backdrop. Suitable for desktop side panels.
doc_drawer_best_practice_1: Use when significant space is needed for detailed information while maintaining the context of the current task.
doc_drawer_best_practice_2: Best for scenarios where users need to frequently refer to information or compare multiple items.
doc_drawer_best_practice_3: Always provide a clear title for the content being displayed and an easy way to close the drawer.
doc_hamburgermenu_long_desc: The HamburgerMenu is a component for storing navigation items, primarily for mobile devices.
doc_hamburgermenu_mobile_only_desc: This component is primarily designed for mobile devices (narrow screen widths). For desktop environments, consider using Navbar or Sidebar.
doc_hamburgermenu_title: Collapsible navigation menu for mobile devices
doc_hovercard_desc: Card that displays detailed information on hover
doc_hovercard_feat_complexity_desc: Tooltips handle only short text, while HoverCards can handle rich content (images, links, multiple paragraphs, etc.).
doc_hovercard_feat_delay_desc: To prevent accidental triggers, a longer display delay (default 700ms) is typically set compared to Tooltips.
doc_hovercard_feat_interaction_desc: HoverCards are designed for hovering over the displayed content itself or clicking links within it.
doc_hovercard_feat_interaction_rich: Enhanced interactivity including links, media, and nested buttons.
doc_hovercard_long_desc: A component to display additional information when hovered.
doc_hovercard_pc_only_desc: This component is designed primarily for PCs as it assumes pointer hover operations. It may not work as intended on touch devices.
doc_hovercard_title: A floating card that reveals additional information or previews when hovering over an element.
doc_hovercard_vs_tooltip: HoverCard is similar to Tooltip, but differs in the following ways:
doc_loadingoverlay_a11y_1: The component includes `role="status"` and `aria-live="polite"` for screen reader announcements.
doc_loadingoverlay_a11y_2: The `aria-busy="true"` attribute indicates that the content is loading.
doc_loadingoverlay_a11y_3: Ensure that loading messages are descriptive and helpful for users relying on assistive technologies.
doc_loadingoverlay_basic_desc: Mask overlay used to prevent interactions while content or pages are loading.
doc_loadingoverlay_bp_choose_appropriate: Use absolute positioning for partial page updates and fixed positioning for full-page operations.
doc_loadingoverlay_bp_consider_backdrop_: Use light backdrops on dark backgrounds and vice versa for better visibility.
doc_loadingoverlay_bp_provide_context_de: Include a message when the loading operation might take more than a few seconds.
doc_loadingoverlay_bp_timeout_handling_d: Always implement timeout handling to prevent indefinite loading states.
doc_loadingoverlay_bp_use_sparingly_desc: Only show loading overlays when necessary to avoid disrupting the user experience.
doc_loadingoverlay_desc: A component that overlays the entire screen or a specific element to display a loading state with customizable loaders, backdrops, and messages.
doc_loadingoverlay_ex_backdrop: Light and dark backdrop options for different background colors.
doc_loadingoverlay_ex_basic: A simple loading overlay with the default spinner.
doc_loadingoverlay_ex_blur: Control the backdrop blur intensity: none, sm, md, or lg.
doc_loadingoverlay_ex_custom: Provide custom content instead of the default loaders.
doc_loadingoverlay_ex_full: Use  ixed={true} to create a full-screen loading overlay.
doc_loadingoverlay_ex_interactive: A practical example showing how to toggle the loading state.
doc_loadingoverlay_ex_message: Display a message along with the loading indicator.
doc_loadingoverlay_ex_types: Choose from various loader animations: spinner, bars, dots, or pulse.
doc_loadingoverlay_feat_aria_desc: Provides necessary ARIA attributes for screen reader accessibility during loading.
doc_loadingoverlay_feat_backdrop_desc: Light or dark backdrop with adjustable blur effects.
doc_loadingoverlay_feat_custom_desc: Replace the default loader with custom content.
doc_loadingoverlay_feat_message_desc: Display a loading message to provide context to users.
doc_loadingoverlay_feat_position_desc: Can be positioned absolutely within a container or fixed for full-screen coverage.
doc_loadingoverlay_feat_types_desc: Choose from spinner, bars, dots, or pulse animations.
doc_loadingoverlay_long_desc: Details and usage for the Loading Overlay component.
doc_loadingoverlay_title: Overlay to cover containers and indicate a loading state
doc_loadingoverlay_wtu_1: When you need to block user interaction during asynchronous operations like data fetching or form submission.
doc_loadingoverlay_wtu_2: When you want to provide visual feedback that a process is ongoing.
doc_loadingoverlay_wtu_3: When you need a full-screen loading indicator for application initialization.
doc_loadingoverlay_wtu_4: When you want to overlay a specific section of the page during partial updates.
doc_menu_a11y_1: Keyboard navigation support (focus moves with Tab key)
doc_menu_comp_divider_desc: Inserts a divider between menu items.
doc_menu_comp_itemgroup_desc: Groups menu items with a displayed title.
doc_menu_comp_menu_desc: Root component managing display modes and open/closed states.
doc_menu_comp_menuitem_desc: Individual menu items. Supports click events and icons.
doc_menu_comp_submenu_desc: Expandable submenus. Requires a unique itemKey.
doc_menu_long_desc: A versatile menu component for navigation or action lists. Supports submenus, grouping, and multiple display modes.
doc_menu_props_default_open_keys: Array of keys for submenu opened by default
doc_menu_stories_groups_desc: Example of grouping menu items, also using dividers.
doc_menu_stories_horizontal_desc: Horizontal menu suitable for header navigation.
doc_menu_stories_icons_desc: Example of menu items with icons.
doc_menu_stories_inline_desc: Inline mode menu suitable for sidebar navigation.
doc_menu_stories_submenu_desc: Example of a menu with submenus. Submenus expand/collapse on click.
doc_notification_best_practice_1: Use for information that is more important than a toast and needs to be clearly communicated, such as background task completions or important system updates.
doc_notification_best_practice_2: Provide a clear title and concise description to help users understand what happened and if any action is needed.
doc_notification_best_practice_3: Be mindful of screen real estate; avoid showing too many notifications at once as they might block important UI elements.
doc_notification_long_desc: Display a notification message globally in a corner of the system.
doc_notification_usage_desc: Used for system-level dynamic messages that are more complex or important than simple toasts.
doc_popover_feat_interaction_complex: Supports focus trapping and complex nested components like forms.
doc_popover_best_practice_1: Use for displaying supplementary information or simple input forms without a full page transition.
doc_popover_best_practice_2: Make the trigger element clear and ensure the popover can be easily dismissed by clicking outside or via an explicit close button.
doc_popover_best_practice_3: Keep the content within the popover focused; consider using a Dialog for more complex layouts or multi-step processes.
doc_snackbar_adv_desc: When managing snackbars across the entire application, using a Provider is recommended.
doc_snackbar_long_desc: A notification component that temporarily displays processing results at the bottom of the screen.
doc_snackbar_title: A small message that appears briefly at the bottom of the screen to provide feedback.
doc_snackbar_usage_action_desc: Can include an action button that the user can interact with.
doc_snackbar_usage_close_desc: Displays a button that allows the user to manually close the snackbar.
doc_snackbar_usage_interactive_desc: You can see it in action.
doc_snackbar_var_desc: Use the `variant` property to apply styles based on state (Success, Error, etc.).
doc_toast_basic_usage_desc: Toasts combine a title, description, and variant (info, success, warning, error).
doc_toast_desc: Non-intrusive floating messages that provide feedback on system operations
doc_toast_best_practice_1: Use to notify users of temporary, low-priority process results like 'Saved' or 'Deleted'.
doc_toast_best_practice_2: For critical errors requiring user action or confirmation, consider using Dialog or Alert.
doc_toast_best_practice_3: Keep messages short and concise, and limit the number of toasts displayed simultaneously on screen.
doc_toast_interactive_example_desc: In the example below, you can interact with a toast to see how it works.
doc_toast_long_desc: The toast component is used to convey short notifications or feedback to the user. It usually appears in the corner of the screen and disappears automatically after a certain period of time.
doc_toast_provider_prop_children_desc: Children elements to be rendered within the provider
doc_toast_provider_prop_duration_desc: Duration for which the toast is displayed (in milliseconds)
doc_toast_provider_prop_position_desc: Toast display position (top-right, top-left, top-center, bottom-right, bottom-left, bottom-center)
doc_toast_system_usage: How to use the Toast System
doc_toast_system_usage_desc: To actually display a toast, it is recommended to wrap your application in a `ToastProvider` and use the `useToast` hook.
doc_toast_title: Non-intrusive floating messages that provide feedback on system operations
doc_toast_variants_desc: You can use the `variant` property to select a style appropriate for the type of notification.
doc_tooltip_delay_desc: You can set the delay time before the tooltip is displayed using the `delay` property (Default: 200ms).
doc_tooltip_long_desc: Tooltips are used to provide context and explanations for UI elements, supplementing information without interrupting user actions.
doc_tooltip_best_practice_1: Use for displaying supplementary explanations for icons or short text.
doc_tooltip_best_practice_2: Do not include interactive elements like links or buttons within a tooltip. Use a Popover for that instead.
doc_tooltip_best_practice_3: Keep text concise and ensure that important information can be understood even without the tooltip.
doc_tour_desc: Sequential guided walkthroughs to onboard users or highlight features
doc_tour_feature_desc: `Tour` displays step-by-step instructions while highlighting specific elements. Ideal for onboarding new users or introducing new features.
doc_tour_long_desc: A guide component to walk users through features or screen operations.
doc_tour_title: Sequential guided walkthroughs to onboard users or highlight features
doc_tour_usage_desc: Specify element selectors, titles, and descriptions for each step in the `steps` prop. Control the tour visibility with the `open` prop.
